sections.paragraph: m., Ezr 1:8, a treasurer, the officer who had the charge of the royal treasures amongst the Persians, see under גְּנַז. Pl. Ch. נִּזַּבְרִין Ezr 7:21 and by extruding the sibilant, גְּדָבְרִין Dan 3:2, to this answers the Syriac ܓܺܙܒܰܪܐܳ, ܓܝܺܙܒܰܪܐܳ, ܓܰܢܢܙܘܳܪܳܐ, Pers. كنحوار, all of which are composed of גַּז, גְּנַז and the Persic syllable وار, ور (Germ. bar, in ehrbar, achtbar), which is used to form possessives.
